Coordinates pre-contract tracking and cross-departmental document distribution.

project_executivo_received -> save_to_drive -> notify_takeoff -> takeoff_complete -> notify_budget -> budget_review -> camila_review -> approval
design_book_assembly -> floor_plans -> elevations -> spec_sheet -> 3d_renderings -> material_selections -> distribute

core_knowledge.description: Lakeshore maintains a portfolio of 27+ standardized home models (spec homes) including Palmer, Dominica, and others. Each model has standardized specifications: door sizes, roof height, flooring, areas, kitchens, cabinets. Models have budget templates that vary by county (Marion, Citrus, Charlotte). A single model can be replicated up to 50 times.

Spec homes follow simplified flow: strategic meeting -> design -> standard budget by county -> released for sale. Custom homes require individual design, pre-agreement payment from client, unique budget creation, and extensive client interaction. Custom clients frequently change specifications (finishes, floors, cabinets) making budget a moving target.

Full lifecycle: 1) Client Contact, 2) Concept Meeting (Day 1 tracking), 3) Design Development (~1 week to first proposal), 4) Pre-Agreement (financial commitment for full design), 5) Full Design (structural, elevations, 3D), 6) Engineering (external engineer returns PDF), 7) Budget Creation (Samuel/team), 8) Client Approval (condensed budget format), 9) Contract Signing, 10) Execution Handoff to Cris.

The design book is a critical output document containing: floor plans, elevations, specification sheets, 3D renderings, material selections. Spec homes have standardized design books per model with variations only for elevations. Custom homes have unique evolving design books. Used by Budget, Permits, Field, and Client Relations.

Custom home budgets use allowances (provisao/verba estimada) for items with unknown exact cost, such as finishing materials, lighting, and fixtures. The budget presented to clients shows condensed categories (e.g., 'flooring') rather than individual line items. This creates a gap between client-facing and internal budget views. Allowances are defined by Camila and Felipe before contract signing.

Model 1774 (1,774 sqft) is a reference spec home with 19 cost categories, 95 services, and 206 cost line items. Categories span from Contract through Lot Variances. This model serves as a template for understanding the cost structure of all Lakeshore models.

Client-requested design changes trigger cascading impacts across departments: Takeoff (re-quantify affected items), Budget (revise cost estimates), Scheduling (assess timeline impact), Permits (structural changes may need permit revision), Compras (adjust POs), Field (distribute updated plans). The team must assess feasibility based on construction stage, engineering recalculation needs, and permit implications.

daily_workflows.name: Custom Home Design Development
steps: Receive client request via Camila, Bernardo, or Juliana
Schedule concept meeting with client
Start tracking in ClickUp (Day 1)
Develop architectural plan in AutoCAD (~1 week to first proposal)
Present proposal to client
If approved: present pre-agreement (fee for full design development)
Full design: structural plans, elevations, 3D renderings (team collaboration)
Luana sends project to external engineer
Receive project executivo PDF from engineer
Route to Budget team (Samuel/Felipe) for takeoff and costing
Budget returns to Camila for client presentation (condensed format)
Client approves budget -> request construction contract
critical_decisions: Allowance vs fixed value classification for finishing items
Whether structural changes require engineering recalculation

daily_workflows.name: Spec Home Model Development
steps: Strategic meeting with Camila, Bernardo, Samuel, and Leo
Develop new model plans in AutoCAD/SketchUp
Standardize specifications: doors, roof height, floors, areas, kitchens, cabinets
Send to Budget team for standard budget creation
Budget template created with county variations (Marion, Citrus, Charlotte)
Model released for sale after budget finalization
critical_decisions: Portfolio strategy: which new models to develop

daily_workflows.name: Document Management and Distribution
steps: Store PDFs in Google Drive 'administration' folder
Attach documents to Lakeshore standard contract package
Organize by phase: Pre-contract (survey, client data, property data), Contract (budget, spec sheet, design book, plans, draw schedule)
Mark superseded versions in Google Drive
Notify affected parties of document updates (manual process)
critical_decisions: Which departments need notification of document changes
Whether floor plan update affects permits (triggers re-review)

daily_workflows.name: 3D Rendering and Visualization
steps: Model project in 3D using SketchUp
Apply materials and lighting in Enscape or Lumion
Generate final renderings
Create humanized floor plans (Gabriela/Candida using Photoshop)
Distribute for client presentation and marketing via Google Drive
daily_workflows.frequency: Per new model or custom project
critical_decisions: Material selection accuracy for renderings matching actual specifications

features_used: Portfolio Management (/models) - browse draft and ready models
Contract Development (/contract_development) - initialize and manage contracts from ready models
Model 1774 reference: READY status with 95 services and 206 cost line items
api_endpoints: /models - GET list of models, POST create new model
/models/{id} - GET model details including cost structure

features_used: Document upload and versioning per project
Design book organization (floor plans, elevations, spec sheets, renderings)

handoffs.trigger: Floor Plan Complete - design documents finalized and project executivo received from engineer
handoffs.data_exchanged: Floor plan PDF, design book, spec sheet, model type, county assignment, project specifications
handoffs.impact: Critical handoff that triggers four parallel downstream workflows: permit application, takeoff quantification, budget creation, and scheduling preparation. Any delay here blocks the entire construction pipeline.

handoffs.trigger: Design book and takeoff ready for budget creation
handoffs.data_exchanged: Plans, spec sheets, design books, allowance vs fixed value definitions, county-specific requirements
handoffs.impact: Budget cannot be created without design specifications. For custom homes, allowance definitions directly affect contract pricing.

handoffs.data_exchanged: Signed contract + all design documents (design book, spec sheet, floor plan, draw schedule)
handoffs.impact: Construction cannot begin without complete execution handoff package

handoffs.trigger: Project status changes (design stages, budget ready, contract status)
handoffs.data_exchanged: Project status information, design previews, rendering images for client communication
handoffs.impact: Client Relations needs project status for customer communication and expectations management

handoffs.data_exchanged: Updated floor plans, design specifications, change details
handoffs.impact: Field must have current plans to avoid building with outdated specifications

handoffs.trigger: Architecture design complete -> send for engineering; Engineer returns project executivo
handoffs.impact: Engineering turnaround directly affects project timeline; executivo PDF is prerequisite for takeoff

pain_points.description: No pre-contract tracking system exists. No way to formally track new project leads, design deliveries, client approvals, and cancellations from initial contact to contract signing.
pain_points.current_workaround: Camila tracks informally via ClickUp and personal notes; no company-wide visibility
pain_points.proposed_solution: Pre-contract pipeline module with stages (Lead, Concept, Pre-Agreement, Design, Budget, Contract) and full visibility for management

pain_points.description: Poor change management for design revisions. Revisions during active construction are not well tracked or communicated to field and project managers. Risk of building with outdated plans.
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual notification via email/Slack; field may not receive updates in time
pain_points.proposed_solution: Automated revision notification system with mandatory acknowledgement from all affected departments before construction can proceed with updated plans

pain_points.description: Company CPUs inadequate for architecture software. AutoCAD, SketchUp, Enscape, and Lumion require GPU-intensive machines that company laptops cannot handle.
pain_points.current_workaround: Gabriela and Candida use personal computers; installation/licensing issues with company devices
pain_points.proposed_solution: Procure workstation-class laptops with dedicated GPUs for all architecture staff; cloud rendering option for Enscape/Lumion

pain_points.description: 3,500 items to update manually between models. When a model design changes, all affected takeoffs, specifications, and budgets must be updated item by item with no template or duplication mechanism.
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual update of each item one by one; error-prone and time-consuming
pain_points.proposed_solution: Model inheritance system: parent model with derived variants. Changes to parent propagate automatically to children with delta review and approval.

pain_points.description: Custom client budget presented condensed (categories not line items) creates gap between client expectations and internal tracking. Allowances add further ambiguity.
pain_points.current_workaround: Camila manages expectations verbally; detailed internal budget maintained separately
pain_points.proposed_solution: Dual-view budget system: client-facing condensed view and internal detailed view, automatically synchronized

pain_points.description: Architecture team works in isolation with little cross-departmental interaction. Field should participate in design review; scheduling should be aware of design complexity.
pain_points.current_workaround: Issues discovered during construction rather than design phase
pain_points.proposed_solution: Design review meetings with field supervisors for complex projects; digital review board for cross-department feedback

pain_points.description: Floor plan updates sometimes not received by Permits team. When Camila/Felipe add or update plans, the permits team should receive them automatically but currently does not.
pain_points.current_workaround: Manual forwarding of updated documents to Permits; sometimes forgotten
pain_points.proposed_solution: Automated document distribution: when floor plan is updated in project folder, all subscribed departments receive notification and access to new version

tribal_knowledge.description: Camila holds the institutional knowledge of Lakeshore's design standards, including standard door sizes, roof heights, kitchen layouts, cabinet specifications, and finishing level definitions (Level 1-4 for spec homes). She also knows the rationale behind each standard and when exceptions are acceptable.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: Critical - design consistency across all models would be lost; no documented standard exists for many specifications

tribal_knowledge.topic: Allowance vs Fixed Value Classification Logic
tribal_knowledge.description: The rules for when to use an allowance (estimated budget provision) vs a fixed value in custom home budgets are undocumented. Camila and Felipe decide based on: how far along the client is in material selection, whether the material category is volatile in price, and historical experience with similar custom projects.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: High - incorrect classification leads to either contract disputes (fixed value too low) or client confusion (too many allowances)

tribal_knowledge.description: Same model has variations by county due to different wind speed requirements (120 mph Marion vs 140-150 mph Charlotte), different utility providers, different inspection requirements, and different permit fees. Camila and the team know which specifications change per county and which remain constant.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: High - models may be built with wrong specifications for the county, creating compliance issues

tribal_knowledge.description: Knowledge of preferred engineers for different project types, their turnaround times, pricing, and common revision requests. Includes understanding of which engineers handle residential vs commercial and which respond faster for urgent projects.
tribal_knowledge.risk_if_lost: Medium - would need to rebuild relationships and learn new engineers' workflows

compliance_requirements.requirement: All projects must have a signed pre-agreement before full design development begins for custom homes
compliance_requirements.regulation: Internal policy - financial commitment before design investment
compliance_requirements.verification: Pre-agreement document signed and filed in project folder before design hours are logged

compliance_requirements.requirement: Design documents must be versioned with superseded copies clearly marked
compliance_requirements.verification: Google Drive folder shows clear versioning; no duplicate active versions exist

compliance_requirements.requirement: Floor plan must be sent to Permits before permit application can begin
compliance_requirements.regulation: County building code requirement - architectural plans required for permit
compliance_requirements.verification: Permits team confirms receipt of current floor plan version before submission

compliance_requirements.requirement: Structural changes to plans require engineering review before implementation
compliance_requirements.regulation: Florida Building Code - structural modifications must be engineer-approved
compliance_requirements.verification: Updated project executivo PDF from engineer on file before structural change proceeds

compliance_requirements.requirement: Wind speed specifications must match county requirements (120 mph Marion, 140-150 mph Charlotte)
compliance_requirements.regulation: Florida Building Code wind speed zones
compliance_requirements.verification: Engineering calculations reference correct wind speed for project county

key_metrics.target: < 8 weeks from concept meeting to contract signing
key_metrics.measurement: Days from first client meeting to signed contract date

key_metrics.target: Maintain 27+ active models with budget templates
key_metrics.measurement: Count of models with READY status and approved budget templates

key_metrics.target: < 2 weeks from plan submission to project executivo return
key_metrics.measurement: Days from Luana sending to engineer to PDF receipt

key_metrics.target: < 3 business days to assess and communicate revision impact to all departments
key_metrics.measurement: Days from revision request to all department notifications sent

key_metrics.target: 100% of custom home leads tracked from contact to contract/cancellation
key_metrics.measurement: Percentage of custom leads with complete stage tracking in system

key_metrics.target: Zero incidents of outdated documents reaching field
key_metrics.measurement: Count of incidents where field received non-current document version
